CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INTEGER, test_string VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (1, 'test test'), (2, 'string without match'), (3, ''), (4, 'search string'), (5, 'another test'), (6, 'test');
SELECT id, instr(test_string, 'es') AS position FROM test_strings WHERE instr(test_string, 'es') > 0;
SELECT id, instr(test_string, 'match') AS position FROM test_strings WHERE instr(test_string, 'match') = 0;
SELECT id, instr(test_string, 'string') FROM test_strings WHERE instr(test_string, 'string') >= 1;
SELECT id, instr(test_string, 't') AS position FROM test_strings ORDER BY position;
SELECT t1.id, instr(t1.test_string, t2.search_string) FROM test_strings t1 JOIN (SELECT 'test' AS search_string) t2 ON instr(t1.test_string, t2.search_string) > 0;
SELECT id, instr(test_string, 'test') + instr(test_string, 'search') AS total_position FROM test_strings;
SELECT instr('abcabcabc', 'abc') AS position1, instr('abcabcabc', 'd') AS position2;
SELECT t1.id, instr(t1.test_string, t2.search_string) FROM test_strings t1 CROSS JOIN (SELECT 'test' AS search_string) t2;
SELECT t1.id, instr(t1.test_string, t2.search_string) FROM test_strings t1 JOIN (SELECT 'another' AS search_string) t2 ON instr(t1.test_string, t2.search_string) = 0;
SELECT id, test_string, instr(test_string, term) FROM test_strings CROSS JOIN search_terms;
